title: Maren Kelsome
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|the soldier Maren Kelsome|the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Marenn Yagen]]}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:KelsomeGG3_1stEd.jpg]] |name=Maren Kelsome |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]][[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height=1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Outpost Beta here. I have a visual now. These things look like…animals. They're huge, maybe ten, fifteen meters high, four-legged, and, and their heads move. They're made out of metal. They have extremely heavy armor and armament. I—I count six of 'em so far.|Maren Kelsome reports the first sighting of AT-AT walkers|{{ESBRadio|Fire and Ice}}}} '''Maren Kelsome''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Rebel trooper/Legends|soldier]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. As a young man, Kelsome received his first assignment as a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] to the frozen [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Akuria II]] to clean out the remnants of a gutted [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Polar Base|garrison]] destroyed during the [[Mission to Akuria II]], just [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Kelsome performed adequately, helping to remove the last Imperial [[Troop/Legends|troops]], which allowed the native [[Akurian]]s to regain their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Odan]], commander of the Rebels on Akuria II, promoted Kelsome from [[Private/Legends|private]] to [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]] for his performance. Kelsome later answered a call for soldiers trained in arctic survival to protect the Alliance's [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the ice planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], where, under the command of Colonel [[Ledick Firest/Legends|Ledick Firest]], he learned how to function in a formal military structure, which he found quite different from the ragtag Akuria II group that had served under Odan. During the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Kelsome acted as Echo Base's forward scout at [[Outpost Beta/Legends|Outpost Beta]] on the base's defensive perimeter, designated '''Point Rider Five'''. He earned the distinction of being the first to report the sighting of Imperial [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] approaching the base from Hoth's [[North Ridge/Legends|North Ridge]], but he and his [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]] mount were crushed to [[Death/Legends|death]] under an AT-AT's massive footpad after underestimating the [[Walker/Legends|walkers]]' deceptive speed. ==Biography== ===First assignment and transfer to Echo Base=== Young Maren Kelsome's first assignment with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] was to the ice [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Akuria II]] as a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] under the command of [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Odan]]. Kelsome was responsible for sweeping up retreating [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Troop/Legends|troops]] from a gutted [[Polar Base|garrison]] that Odan and his ragtag forces previously wiped out with the help of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] during a [[Mission to Akuria II|battle]] just [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].''[[The Kingdom of Ice]]'' Kelsome performed adequately, removing the Imperial presence, and helping the native [[Akurian]]s to reclaim their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Odan promoted Kelsome from [[Private/Legends|private]] to [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]] for his actions. When the call for [[Rebel trooper/Legends|soldiers]] trained in arctic survival to protect the Alliance's new [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] reached Odan, Kelsome volunteered and transferred to the ice planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. At Echo Base, Kelsome served under field commander Colonel [[Ledick Firest/Legends|Ledick Firest]], who taught Kelsome formal military structure, which he found to be quite a departure from Odan's undisciplined group on Akuria II. Kelsome was assigned to one of the regular scouting patrols around Echo Base's outer perimeter.''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ===Battle of Hoth=== {{Quote|Those walkers will pick up your presence any time now. You and your men get out of there on the double, understand me, son?''"
"''Halfway home, General! Outpost Beta out.|General Carlist Rieekan and Maren Kelsome|}} [[File:MarenKelsomeATAT-SW1977-40.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Maren Kelsome and his tauntaun were crushed to death by an AT-AT.]] With the onset of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Kelsome was one of the base's many soldiers who were thrust into first-time combat duty. He served as the Alliance's forward scout at [[Outpost Beta/Legends|Outpost Beta]], the outermost manned station on the base's perimeter, under the designation Point Rider Five.[[Star Wars (1977) 40|''Star Wars'' (1977) 40]] When the Empire arrived in the [[Hoth system/Legends|Hoth system]], Kelsome and the other advance outposts were notified, with instructions to observe and relay any information on the Imperial invasion force they could, and then to fall back to avoid being overrun. Kelsome was the first to report the presence of Imperial [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] two [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] from Hoth's [[North Ridge/Legends|North Ridge]], although he didn't know what the giant machines were. After issuing an initial [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] report to [[Echo Station 3-T-8/Legends|Echo Station 3-T-8]], he nervously described the physical specifications of the heavily armored [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] via [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] directly to [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] in the Echo Base command center. Rieekan grimly determined that Kelsome was describing the formidable AT-AT walker, and, after Kelsome warned that the walkers would be in firing range of the perimeter in another [[Standard minute/Legends|minute]], Rieekan ordered him and his men to vacate immediately.[[The Empire Strikes Back: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''The Empire Strikes Back'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]] Kelsome, however, underestimated the deceptive speed of the AT-AT while retreating back to the base. After issuing one final warning of the AT-AT advance to the Echo Base defenses over the whine of servomotors, [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] blasts, and the terrified screams of his [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]], Kelsome and his mount were crushed to [[Death/Legends|death]] under one of the walkers' massive footpads before the Alliance fired a single shot. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You want to live forever?|Maren Kelsome|}} Maren Kelsome's arctic survival skills validated him for transfer from one ice planet—Akuria II—to another—Hoth—to serve at the newly-constructed Echo Base. While under the tutelage of Ledick Firest, Kelsome found stringent military protocol to be very different from the loose group run by Odan on Akuria II. Kelsome was unfamiliar with the AT-AT walker, failing to recognize the giant machine for what it was when reporting the first sighting of the approaching Imperial force to Echo Base during the Battle of Hoth. The imposing size of the war machine frightened the youthful Kelsome, and his nervousness was evident during his comlink conversation with Carlist Rieekan. His unfamiliarity with the AT-AT would prove to be his undoing, when he failed to recognize the walker's deceptive speed and was consequently trampled to death. During his scout service on Hoth, Kelsome was trained in tauntaun riding, demolitions, and [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] use. ==Equipment== Kelsome's equipment included a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]], a comlink, and [[Macrobinoculars/Legends|macrobinoculars]]. ==Behind the scenes== ===Character origins and development=== [[File:MCQ-atat.jpg|thumb|right|300px|This Ralph McQuarrie concept illustration served as the inspiration for the creation of the Maren Kelsome character.]] A "Corporal Kelsome" character first appeared in the [[1989]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' to provide a profile of a standard Echo Base soldier for [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] purposes. The Kelsome character was provided a detailed backstory in the [[1996]] [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|second edition]] of ''Galaxy Guide 3'', as part of a major expansion on the first edition sourcebook by [[Pablo Hidalgo]], which also provided a full name for the character, "Maren Kelsome." The second edition of ''Galaxy Guide 3'' tied Kelsome with a tauntaun rider appearing in early versions of ''The Empire Strikes Back'' script, who first encounters the AT-AT walkers during the opening stages of the Battle of Hoth. This tauntaun rider, before being identified as Kelsome, first appeared in the [[1980]] [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] adaptation of the film, [[Star Wars (1977) 40|''Star Wars'' (1977) 40]], and also appears with several lines in the [[1983]] [[The Empire Strikes Back (radio)|radio adaptation]] of ''The Empire Strikes Back'', identified only as "Outpost Beta." With the merging of these two characters, the second edition of ''Galaxy Guide 3'' was the first source to confirm Kelsome's death in the battle. Kelsome's biographical entry in the second edition of ''Galaxy Guide 3'' also links him to the ''Marvel'' serial comic ''[[The Kingdom of Ice]]'', released from [[July]] [[1978]] to [[January]] [[1979]]. Specific references in the entry, including the planet Akuria II, the native Akurians, the character Colonel Odan, and a mention of the Imperial garrison defeated during the Mission to Akuria II, all originated in the ''Marvel'' comic. The "tauntaun rider" character destined to become Kelsome was first illustrated by conceptual artist [[Ralph McQuarrie]] during the development stages of ''The Empire Strikes Back''. Originally, the rider was intended to be Luke Skywalker, who, in the conceptualized scene, rides his tauntaun up a ravine only to be surprised by the presence of Imperial walkers and then must flee for his life.''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' ===Character confusion=== Maren Kelsome should not be confused with the character of [[Trey Callum/Legends|Trey Callum]], who appears in the film as the trench [[Officer/Legends|officer]] who confirms the presence of AT-ATs approaching Echo Base. Callum is the third link in a chain of comlink calls, originating with Kelsome and passing through Echo Station 3-T-8, that reports the presence of AT-ATs to the base.{{InsiderCite|74|Who's Who in Echo Base: The Unsung Rebels of the Battle of Hoth}}{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1545#16926420|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2007-09-26|time=5:34 PM}} The [[1990]] West End Games ''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'' and the sourcebook's [[1994]] [[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|second edition]] include an introductory narrative in "Chapter Six: Ground Combat" detailing how "the advance scout on Echo Base's North Ridge [who was] the first to see the walkers" goes on to save the life of a downed [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeder]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] once the last AT-AT has passed his position. The scout single-handedly neutralizes a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Cold assault stormtrooper|snowtroopers]] before carrying the pilot to safety on his tauntaun. This description is a clear reference to the "tauntaun rider" who was later identified as Maren Kelsome. However, with the advent of Kelsome's death early in the battle as established by ''Galaxy Guide 3'' in 1996, any connection between this character in the ''Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'' and Kelsome provides a stark [[Canon|continuity]] inconsistency, since Kelsome is long dead by this point.''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] ===Inconsistencies=== In ''The Empire Strikes Back'' radio drama and the radio drama script, published in ''[[The Empire Strikes Back: The National Public Radio Dramatization]]'', Carlist Rieekan addresses Kelsome as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]]. The first edition ''Galaxy Guide 3'' later identified Kelsome as being a corporal during the Battle of Hoth. Kelsome's report of the AT-ATs in the radio drama script describes the walkers as "twenty, twenty-five [[Meter/Legends|meters]] high," whereas in the actual radio drama, the Kelsome character describes the walkers as "ten, fifteen meters high." This height differential mirrors a persistent canonical discrepancy across sources concerning the actual height of the AT-AT walkers. To date, many sources, including West End Games ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' supplements and sourcebooks such as ''[[Star Wars: Complete Cross-Sections]]'', identify the AT-AT at 15.5 meters,''[[Star Wars: Complete Cross-Sections]]'' while others, including [[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']], put the AT-AT at 22.5 meters.[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']] ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'' {{C|Hoth concept art game extra}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 40|''Star Wars'' (1977) 40]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 40}} {{1st}} {{Ret}} *{{ESBRadio|Fire and Ice}} {{1stID|Beta}} ==Sources== *''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' {{Po}} {{C|Identified as Luke Skywalker}} *''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' {{1stm}} {{C|as "Corporal Kelsome"}} *''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' {{Co}} *''[[From Star Wars to Indiana Jones: The Best of the Lucasfilm Archives]]'' *[[The Empire Strikes Back: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''The Empire Strikes Back'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]] {{ID|Beta}} *[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] {{1stID|Maren}} *{{InsiderCite|74|Who's Who in Echo Base: The Unsung Rebels of the Battle of Hoth}} {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{Topps|set=2015 Topps Star Wars Illustrated: The Empire Strikes Back|cardname=Walkers on the North Ridge|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/112358/cid/7720175/2015-Topps-Star-Wars-Illustrated-The-Empire-Strikes-Back-34-Walkers-on-the-North-Ridge}} {{Imo}} {{C|as Outpost Beta}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Maren Kelsome }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kelsome, Maren}} [[Category:Alliance Army personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
